basics.name: Osnat (Ossi) Mokryn
basics.label: Associate Professor of Information Systems, University of Haifa
basics.summary: I study what's missing. My research treats the absence of expected signals as information — an information-theoretic approach (Learning via Surprisability) for representing people and populations, detecting anomalies, and making the unseen visible. I also study how generative AI reshapes human creativity and teamwork, and how the timing of real-world interactions drives contagion and collective behavior. Director of the Social, AI & Networks (SCAN) Lab; Chair of the ACM IUI Steering Committee.

work.name: University of Haifa
work.position: Associate Professor, Department of Information Systems
work.summary: Director, Social, AI & Networks (SCAN) Lab. Chair, M.Sc. program in Human–Computer Interaction.
highlights: PI, Israel Science Foundation grant on the effect of external events on social-network structure
General Motors Global R&D research grants; national data-science and AI initiative grants
Building an ERC Advanced proposal on learning from what is absent

work.name: Harvard University — John A. Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences (SEAS)
work.summary: Research on human–AI collaboration and absence-aware analysis (with the Shaer group).
